bio.intro: Hey! I'm a computer science student and Linux tinkerer who enjoys understanding how systems actually work under the hood.
bio.education: I'm a computer science student who spends an unreasonable amount of time breaking operating systems and then fixing them.
bio.projects_highlight: I run Arch Linux (btw), use Neovim, and believe the terminal is the best UI humanity has produced. Check out my Projects to see my latest experiments.
bio.blog_highlight: I like debugging weird problems, experimenting with DevOps setups, and occasionally fighting with Windows bootloaders.
bio.current_work: Currently focused on Linux systems, infrastructure experiments, and infrastructure-style automation like DockForge.
bio.skills_highlight: I experiment with system debugging, network troubleshooting, and building small CLI utilities. Run Skills to see my tech stack.
bio.history: I've been tinkering with systems for years, moving from simple scripting to complex DevOps automation and kernel-level experimentation.
bio.fun_fact: Fun Fact: I'm probably already poking something that's broken with logs and random shell commands before I even realize it's a 'problem'.
bio.outro: At my core, I love building and breaking things. If you're into systems, low-level stuff, or DevOps, let's talk!

Systems.name: Linux
Systems.desc: Arch Linux (btw), system configuration, kernel tinkering

accomplishments.title: Authored Guide on Android Rooting (Proot vs Chroot)
